frameworks.name: cortical excitability and perceptual learning
frameworks.one_line: Excitability changes index learning-related plasticity in visual cortex

frameworks.name: transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS) neuromodulation
frameworks.one_line: Anodal vs cathodal vs sham DC polarization modulates V1 activity

frameworks.name: motor-cortex stimulation analogy
frameworks.one_line: Motor-learning excitability findings motivate the visual-domain extension

core_question: Does anodal tDCS over V1 enhance visual perceptual learning and shift cortical excitability in healthy humans?
key_assumptions: Paired-stimulation VEP ratios index V1 excitability
Anodal tDCS upregulates and cathodal downregulates cortical excitability
manipulations: tDCS polarity (anodal vs cathodal vs sham) over V1
Pre vs post measurement of phosphene threshold and ps-VEP
key_findings: Anodal tDCS improved visual discrimination learning relative to sham
Phosphene thresholds were reduced after anodal tDCS
Paired-stimulation VEP ratios increased after anodal tDCS
Cathodal tDCS had minor or no effect on learning
interpretations: Anodal tDCS over V1 enhances perceptual learning by increasing cortical excitability
Excitability shifts in V1 may serve as a marker of visual learning effects
